layer_2_essential_oil.blend_rationale: Lemon carries the dominant limonene. Black Pepper matches the mint aroma note. Lemongrass provides Myrcene support. Lavender rounds the blend with a soft finish.

layer_3_music.spotify_prompt: A low-energy session. Forest-floor heaviness pulling toward sleep. Genres: ambient, dub, lo-fi hip hop, deep house. Tempo around 65-90 BPM.

layer_4_tea.name: G2D Tea
layer_4_tea.description: Mint and forest floor infused chamomile blend
method: Heat 8 oz filtered water to 185°F.
Add chamomile tea bag and steep for 5 minutes.
Add fresh mint leaves and let infuse for 1 minute.
Add dried porcini mushroom and let infuse for 1 minute.
Strain into a warm teacup.
Stir in honey and serve.

mocktail.name: G2D Fizz
method: Fill a rocks glass with large ice cubes.
Combine 2 oz fresh mint leaves and 0.75 oz fresh lemon juice in a shaker with ice.
Add 0.5 oz mushroom-infused simple syrup.
Shake for 8 seconds.
Strain into glass over fresh ice.
Top with 2 oz sparkling water and stir gently.
Garnish with mint.

cocktail.name: G2D Sour
cocktail.description: Bourbon-based mint and forest floor cocktail
method: Combine 2 oz bourbon, 1.5 oz fresh mint leaves, and 0.75 oz fresh lemon juice in a shaker.
Add 0.5 oz mushroom-infused simple syrup.
Dry shake for 5 seconds (if using egg white).
Add ice and shake for 10 seconds.
Strain into a chilled coupe.
Garnish with mint.

juice.name: G2D Juice
method: Press 2 cups fresh apple through a cold press.
Press 1 green apple through the press.
Add 0.5 inch fresh ginger root to the press.
Combine all juices in a glass.
Add 0.5 oz raw honey and stir until dissolved.
Pour over ice and serve immediately.

wellness_shot.name: G2D Shot
method: Chill a shot glass in the freezer for 2 minutes.
In a mixing glass, combine 1 oz bourbon with 0.75 oz fresh apple juice.
Add 0.25 oz fresh lemon juice.
Stir with ice for 5 seconds.
Strain into the chilled shot glass and serve immediately.

snack.name: Medjool Dates Stuffed with Goat Cheese
snack.desc: Plump dates filled with herbed chèvre and topped with a walnut half and honey drizzle.

appetizer.name: Wild Mushroom Toast with Thyme
appetizer.desc: Sautéed mixed mushrooms on sourdough with fresh thyme and shaved Parmesan.

main.name: Lamb Tagine with Preserved Lemon
main.desc: Slow-braised lamb with apricots, olives, preserved lemon, and warming spices.

dessert.name: Cardamom Rice Pudding
dessert.desc: Creamy arborio rice pudding with cardamom, rosewater, and crushed pistachios.

cheese.name: Manchego & Membrillo
cheese.desc: Aged Manchego with quince paste, Marcona almonds, and crusty bread.

bath_bomb.base: baking soda, citric acid, cornstarch, Epsom salt
bath_bomb.extras: dried flower petals, oat milk powder

shower_steamer.activation: Place on shower floor away from direct stream. Steam activates essential oils for 5-10 minutes of aromatherapy.

layer_10_incense.burn_method: Place on heat-resistant surface in a ventilated room. Allow 30 seconds for the flame to establish before extinguishing to a smolder.
layer_10_incense.ritual_suggestion: Light before the session begins. Let the smoke settle the room while you prepare your forest-floor space.

layer_11_environment.spatial_setup: Low seating, soft throws, candles at eye level. Minimize overhead lighting.
layer_11_environment.scent_anchor: Essential oil blend via diffuser, started 30 minutes before session

layer_12_breathwork.timing: Begin with the diffuser. Complete all cycles before consumption.
layer_12_breathwork.guidance_text: Close your eyes. Breathe in through the nose for four counts, letting the scent fill your lungs. Hold gently for four. Release slowly through the mouth for eight counts, feeling gravity pull you deeper into your seat with each exhale.

layer_13_narrative.text:
14 drops of lemon carry the dominant Limonene through the room slowly — the way Terrapin Farms intended when they cultivated G2D in Orleans, Humboldt County. The lineage reads Gelato 25 X Dosido, but what arrives in the glass is something that transcends parentage: mint, forest floor, and diesel.

The second oil — Black Pepper — adds 13 drops of brightness that matches the forest floor note in the flower itself. Heavy is not a command here but an invitation, the way the best strains suggest rather than insist. The body settles into physically relaxed with the kind of molecular precision that only terpenes at 2.37% total can deliver.

In the dimly lit room, the essential oil blend has been working for thirty minutes before the flower arrives. The pre-load creates a terpene environment that the cannabis completes rather than initiates — your olfactory system already tuned to the frequency of Limonene, already primed for forest-floor heaviness pulling toward sleep. The third oil adds depth. The fourth oil rounds everything into a finish that feels inevitable rather than constructed.

This is what Terrapin Farms grows in Orleans, Humboldt County: not just cannabis, but context. The indica genetics express through terpenes that speak the same chemical language as bay laurel on a Humboldt hillside. The session settles into its final form — sleepy, unhurried, complete — and the 4 oils that started it are now indistinguishable from the flower that finished it.

layer_14_location.terpene_walk: Begin at SF Botanical Garden — Fragrance Garden, diffuse essential oil blend 30 minutes before arrival. Walk slowly, breathing deeply through the nose to layer forest phytoncides over the pre-loaded terpene profile.

wine.terpene_bridge: Sauvignon Blanc's citrus-forward terpene profile naturally mirrors limonene's bright, uplifting aromatic signature.
wine.tasting_note: Meyer lemon, white grapefruit, herbaceous minerality

wine.terpene_bridge: Viognier carries linalool and limonene, bridging floral and citrus in perfect harmony.

beer.terpene_bridge: Citra hops are named for their extraordinary limonene content — delivering the same bright citrus terpene.
Hefeweizen yeast produces isoamyl acetate (banana) and limonene-adjacent citrus esters.

layer_15_wine_beer.cannabaceae_note: Cannabis and hops (Humulus lupulus) are botanical cousins in the family Cannabaceae, sharing terpene synthesis pathways that produce identical aromatic molecules.
